hypothesis: Including heating energy (via heat capacities and reaction enthalpies) and purification costs (via ML-predicted solubility/volatility of byproducts) in the workflow’s economic metric will make the Ca3SiO5+SiO2 route more cost-effective than CaO+SiO2 (grams Ca2SiO4 per euro) for a 5 kg batch at 1500°C with 60% furnace efficiency when electricity price is ≥0.18 €/kWh, using the same reactant price set as in the study.
